content.full_text:
CAROLE·MIGDEN
A SUPERVISOR WE'LL BE PROUD OF.

February 18, 1992


John Wallace
Legal Division
California FPPC
PO Box 807
Sacramento CA  95804-0807

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I am the treasurer for Supervisor Carole Migden, a county supervisor
for the City and County of San Francisco.

I spoke with you today regarding the current status of regulations
regarding inter- and intra-candidate transfers of campaign funds in
light of the Court of Appeals' decision on the SEIU case two weeks
ago.

Regarding intra-candidate transfers, it is my understanding that
funds can be transferred to a committee established for the purposes
of running for an office not subject to limitations, such as State
Assembly or State Senate, irrespective of whether the committee that
is the source of the funds is itself subject to limitations.  However,
no funds can be transferred into an account subject to limitations,
including "special elections" for State Assembly or State Senate.

Regarding inter-candidate transfers, it is my understanding that
campaign funds can only be transferred to committees not subject to
limitations.

Further, it is my understanding that the above applies assuming
there is no local restriction of transferring campaign funds to
committees not subject to limitations.

Can you please confirm that my understandings as above are correct?

Sincerely,

David Weldy

David Weldy


Carole Migden for Supervisor                           4430 20th St.
[illegible address line]                              San Francisco CA
                                                       94114

sections.question: Regarding intra-candidate transfers, it is my understanding that funds can be transferred to a committee established for the purposes of running for an office not subject to limitations, such as state Assembly or State Senate, irregardless of whether the committee that is the source of the funds is itself subject to limitations. However, no funds can be transferred into an account subject to limitations, including "special elections" for State Assembly or State Senate. Regarding inter-candidate transfers, it is my understanding that campaign funds can only be transferred to committees not subject to limitations. Further, it is my understanding that the above applies assuming there is no local restriction of transferring campaign funds to committees not subject to limitations. Can you please confirm that my understandings as above are correct?
sections.question_synthetic: The treasurer seeks confirmation regarding the legality of intra-candidate and inter-candidate campaign fund transfers under current regulations, particularly following a recent Court of Appeals decision in the SEIU case, and whether these transfers are permissible to committees not subject to contribution limitations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. Document is dated February 18, 1992 and references a recent Court of Appeals decision on the SEIU case. The letter contains the requester's understanding/interpretation of the regulations and asks for confirmation.

Regarding inter-candidate transfers, it is my understanding that campaign funds can only be transferred to committees not subject to limitations. Further, it is my understanding that the above applies assuming there is no local restriction of transferring campaign funds to committees not subject to limitations. Can you please confirm that my understandings as above are correct?

embedding.summary: A campaign treasurer for San Francisco Supervisor Carole Migden requests legal clarification from the FPPC regarding the permissibility of intra-candidate and inter-candidate campaign fund transfers in light of a recent court decision.
